behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes Git ancestry/diff behavior plus real PR-state lookup; pure in-process mocks can hide mismatches between actual git history and PR provider semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo with `main` and a feature branch ahead of `main`; backdate commit timestamps beyond threshold; ensure no PR exists for the branch in the real PR source used by the app; run the detector end-to-end and assert it returns stale-no-PR for that branch.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers real git + real PR-state dependency, which requires System per level definitions.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure can occur in bootstrap wiring, environment detection, and emitted output path; module-level tests can miss broken startup hooks or CLI/session integration.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the actual session-start entrypoint in a repo state known to be stale-no-PR, capture startup output, and assert warning presence plus actionable context (branch name, age, ahead/commit context) for the current branch.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses the real startup path and output surface, not just internal function calls.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: This is a pure mapping function with deterministic input/output and no I/O; real failures are logic regressions at function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the classifier directly with exhaustive/representative branch-state fixtures and assert exact label outputs and determinism across repeated calls.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No external dependencies are required to expose this class of failure.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes `clean` from `has commits but no PR (STALE)` in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on command wiring, rendering, and live repo/PR state integration; mocks risk always-green output formatting and state-join bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in two real scenarios: (a) branch fully represented by PR/merged or no ahead commits, (b) branch ahead of main with no PR and older than threshold; assert rendered status is `clean` in (a) and explicit STALE/no-PR in (b).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Exercises the actual command surface and external state joins, requiring System.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: PR lifecycle states (open/merged) are external-source semantics; mocked states can miss real API/status interpretation bugs and branch-PR association edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Using real repository + PR provider state, create/identify branches with open PR and merged PR, run staleness detection, and assert neither branch is labeled stale-no-PR.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real PR-state interpretation, which is beyond unit/integration mocks for this failure class.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry equivalence after hash changes; mocked commit graphs often miss these graph-level edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, simulate stale branch commits, then resolve via rebase or squash-merge into main (changing hashes), refresh state, and assert detector no longer reports stale-no-PR for the branch.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real git operations and graph semantics that only System-level execution reliably validates.
